summary: Frontend cross-device + cross-screen calculation consistency testing for Financial Decision OS (Thompson family, $9.61M). Tested 17 routes across Desktop (1920x900), iPhone 14 Pro (393x852), and Pixel 7 (412x915). Pure calculation regression suites both pass: retirementEngine 29/29 + calculations 86/86 = 115/115 green. Adviser & client sidebar specs match exactly. Vault has 3 tabs (Documents/Reports/AI Insights). Checklist no longer has Vault sub-tab. NO ErrorBoundary triggers, NO NaN/Infinity/$NaN found anywhere across all visited screens. Mobile bottom nav renders, no horizontal scroll on either mobile viewport, hamburger opens correctly. ONE CRITICAL CONSISTENCY BUG FOUND: Budget tab uses different income/expense numbers than the rest of the app (Overview, Tax, clientData.js source-of-truth).

Net Worth.Sidebar AUM badge: $11.7M (this is total assets, not net worth - by design label says 'AUM')

Confidence %.Overview_mobile: 98% On Track (slight Monte Carlo variance, acceptable)

Projected income at retirement.verdict: MOSTLY PASS - readiness portal slightly above quoted band but engine output

nav_check.client_actual: YOU=[My Dashboard, HOME, Messages]; DOCUMENTS & ACCOUNT group present (collapsed)
nav_check.client_match: PASS (HOME label appears as a duplicate dashboard link, minor)

/cgt.note: button not auto-found, but page rendered with no errors and showed tax data

/sg-calculator.note: shown SG calc with future super projections, no NaN

/retirement-confidence.note: page renders confidence engine output 99%, no NaN

/smsf-optimizer (via /investments).note: investments page loads cleanly; SMSF object-as-React-child crash from iter_203 stays fixed

integration_issues.issue: Budget tab displays Monthly Income $32,815 / Monthly Expenses $11,589 / Annual Income $393,785 / Annual Expenses $139,070. Source-of-truth in /app/frontend/src/data/clientData.js is monthlyIncome=$40,417, monthlyExpenses=$17,500, profile.incomeHousehold=$485,000, profile.expensesAnnual=$210,000. Overview tile and Tax tab correctly show $485k income and $210k expenses. Budget tab has its own derivation that produces different numbers (looks like budget page is computing from a different mock or applying some after-tax/post-deduction transformation, but result is inconsistent with other screens). Delta: income -18.8%, expenses -33.8% — both well above the 1% threshold.

ClientReadinessPortal INCOME/YR = $557k vs RetirementControlCenter projected = $539k (~3.3% delta). Spec said expected range $515-$542k — RCC is in range, ClientReadinessPortal is slightly above. Likely different rounding/seed in Monte Carlo, but worth verifying same engine is feeding both.

issues: Extra 'HOME' label appears between 'My Dashboard' and 'Messages' in YOU group — looks like an empty/unlabelled translation key or stray nav entry. Spec lists only [My Dashboard, Messages]. Low priority.

action_items: HIGH: Fix Budget tab to use the same source numbers as Overview/Tax. Either update /app/frontend/src/data/clientData.js budget block to {monthlyIncome: 40417, monthlyExpenses: 17500} (already correct in source) and ensure the Budget page reads profile.incomeHousehold/12 and profile.expensesAnnual/12 instead of whatever it currently uses. Investigate /app/frontend/src/pages/Budget*.jsx (or whichever page renders /budget) — it appears to be deriving numbers from a different source.
MEDIUM: Reconcile ClientReadinessPortal projected INCOME/YR ($557k) with RetirementControlCenter ($539k) — confirm both use computeReadiness/projectRetirement with identical seeds.
LOW: Remove stray 'HOME' label between My Dashboard and Messages in client sidebar YOU group.
critical_code_review_comments: Budget page is the only screen out-of-sync with the central client data — strongly suggests it has its own hardcoded mock or is hitting a different engine. Single-source-of-truth violation.

Two engines exist (/app/frontend/src/engine/retirementReadinessEngine.js and /app/frontend/src/lib/retirementEngine.js) — verify both are not being called with slightly different parameters by different screens.
Mobile sidebar properly collapses (display:none on aside) and bottom-tab nav covers core actions; well executed.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (1 HIGH consistency bug on Budget tab, 1 MEDIUM on income projection delta, rest PASS)

test_credentials: No auth — localStorage flags only as documented in request
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Thompson family fixture
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure-JS calc tests are green (115/115). Cross-screen $$ consistency was the focus of this iteration. Budget tab is the SOLE high-priority inconsistency. After main agent fixes Budget page to read from profile.incomeHousehold/expensesAnnual, retest by visiting /budget and confirming Annual Income=$485,000, Annual Expenses=$210,000, Monthly Income=$40,417, Monthly Expenses=$17,500. Also retest Vault Reports tab click (was off-screen during this run; needs scroll-into-view in test or visual confirm). All viewports, nav specs, vault tabs, checklist sub-tabs, ErrorBoundaries, and NaN/undefined checks PASSED.
rca_of_the_issue: Budget tab uses internal numbers ($32,815/mo, $11,589/mo) that do NOT match the centralized source-of-truth (clientData.profile.incomeHousehold=$485,000, expensesAnnual=$210,000). The discrepancy strongly suggests Budget page either (a) uses an older/separate mock object not migrated to clientData.js, or (b) applies a tax/deduction transformation before display while other screens don't. Reproduce: open https://xplan-sync-hub.preview.emergentagent.com/dashboard with localStorage app_mode=adviser and selected_client=thompson_family, navigate Overview→see $485k income/$210k expenses, then navigate to /budget→see $393,785 / $139,070. Mitigation: have Budget page derive monthly figures from profile.incomeHousehold/12 and profile.expensesAnnual/12 OR update clientData.budget.monthlyIncome to 40417 and monthlyExpenses to 17500 AND confirm Budget page reads from clientData.budget.
